name: Quesadilla Quiche
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Watkins Salsa and Sour Cream Snack Dip Seasoning
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pie crust according to package directions for a two-crust pie using a 10-inch pie plate.
2. Place one prepared crust in the pan, trimming edges if necessary; set aside.
3. In a medium skillet, cook onions in oil until tender, then remove from heat.
4. Stir in 1 cup chopped tomatoes, olives, garlic powder, chili powder, and black pepper; set aside.
5. In a small bowl, beat together eggs and 1 1/2 tbsp hot pepper sauce, reserving 2 tbsp of this mixture.
6. To the remaining egg mixture, add 1/2 cup shredded Monterey Jack cheese and 1/2 cup cheddar cheese. Stir to combine.
7. Sprinkle the remaining cheese over the bottom of the pie crust-lined pan.
8. Spoon the onion mixture evenly over the cheese.
9. Carefully pour the egg mixture over the cheese, spreading it to cover.
10. Top with the second crust; seal the edges.
11. With a sharp knife, make decorative slits in several places around the crust.
12. Brush the reserved egg mixture over the quiche.
13. Bake at 375 degrees for 45 to 55 minutes, or until golden brown. If the edges become too brown, cover with foil for the last 10-15 minutes.
14. Let stand for 5 minutes.
15. Meanwhile, combine sour cream and Watkins Salsa and Sour Cream Snack Dip Seasoning; mix well; set aside.
16. Combine fresh tomatoes and hot pepper sauce to taste; set aside.
17. Cut the quiche into wedges and serve topped with the sour cream and tomato mixture.
